name: B2B Marketing Podcast
description: The essential resource for B2B marketers.

recent_episodes.title: Episode 208: Gold, Grand Prix and No BS: Inside Workbooks’ winning campaign
recent_episodes.description:
In this week’s episode of the B2B Marketing Podcast, Kavita Singh sat down with Dan Roche and John Cheney from Workbooks to discuss their Gold wins at the 2025 B2B Marketing Awards.
Workbooks won the Gold award for ‘Best brand initiative under 100k’ as well as the ‘Grand Prix’ for their No BS CRM campaign. Joined by Dan Roche, CMO, Workbooks and John Cheney, CEO, Workbooks, this episode covers everything about their award-winning campaign.

recent_episodes.title: Episode 207: Inside award-winning B2B campaigns and what they mean for marketing in 2026
recent_episodes.description:
In this week’s episode of the B2B Marketing Podcast, David Rowlands spoke with Paul Collier, CMO, FunnelFuel; Chris Omotosho, Managing Director, UK, Gravity Global; Nick Watmough, Executive Creative Director, The Croc; and Jon Buckthorp, Commercial Director, Differentiated. 
As we close out 2025, it only felt right to end with a conversation inspired by the year’s standout work. Drawing on insights from across agency, brand and creative leadership, the panel discusses what made this year’s award

recent_episodes.title: Episode 206: How to create high-performing teams and resource networks, with HelloKindred
recent_episodes.description:
In this week’s episode of the B2B Marketing Podcast in association with HelloKindred, Kavita Singh is joined by Roland Glass, Chief Commercial Officer, HelloKindred as well as Sarah Thomas, the Group Chief Marketing Officer, EVP, over at Capgemini.
Off the back of the Propolis Leader’s Forum, they dig into what truly drives high-performing marketing teams, from adaptability and intellectual curiosity to data fluency and modern leadership behaviors. Roland also breaks down how a resource network

score_reason: Covers B2B marketing campaigns and teams but recent episodes focus more on UK marketing awards than agency building or AI strategy.

key_themes: AI integration and strategy in B2B marketing
Building competitive advantage through data and insights
Brand strategy, rebranding, and standing out in crowded markets
Campaign strategy and creative approaches to B2B marketing
Leadership journeys and CMO/marketing director perspectives
enrichment.relevant_episode: Episode 197: Want to integrate AI into your business strategy? We spoke to Jake Bird to find out how — directly addresses AI readiness and strategy integration, which aligns with Andreas' core teaching and speaking focus on practical AI adoption in traditional industries.
enrichment.coverage_gap: How traditional, non-tech industries (food, agriculture, commodity-based businesses) can actually compete using AI and data—the podcast covers AI strategy in tech-forward companies but hasn't featured a guest who's built and scaled a business in highly traditional sectors while leveraging AI.
enrichment.suggested_hook: The guy who sold Canada's largest food marketing agency teaches B2B marketers how commodity boards beat tech companies at their own game: turning data into decisions faster.
